question.text: The inter-Clearing Corporation collateral comprises two components. Which of the following is one of them?

answer.text: Margins as per existing Risk Management Framework (initial margin, ELM, calendar spread margin etc.)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Inter-CC collateral has two components: (a) Margins as per existing RMF and (b) Additional capital based on credit risk from the linked CC. **The Trap:** No exposure is granted on the additional capital component - it is pure risk buffer. **Cross-Exam Context:** The bilateral risk management between CCs is a more advanced topic. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This bilateral collateral ensures a default by one CC does not cause financial loss to the other.
